daily_summary.headline: Aurora Storms and Routine Launches Highlight March 19

top_events.title: Aurora Viewing Opportunity
top_events.description: A G2 geomagnetic storm is expected, offering a good chance to view auroras at latitudes down to 55°.
top_events.action: Check local weather and plan for aurora viewing.

top_events.title: Zhuque-2E Launch
top_events.description: Zhuque-2E rocket launching with an unknown payload from Jiuquan Satellite Launch Center.

top_events.title: Falcon 9 Starlink Launch
top_events.description: SpaceX Falcon 9 launching Starlink Group 10-33 satellites from Cape Canaveral.
top_events.action: Follow live stream for real-time coverage.

alerts.title: G2 Geomagnetic Storm Alert
alerts.message: A G2 geomagnetic storm is expected on March 19, 2026, with a Kp index reaching up to 6. This provides a good opportunity for aurora viewing at latitudes down to 55°.
alerts.action_required: Plan for aurora viewing if skies are clear.

opportunities.recommendation: Good viewing opportunity for high-latitude observers. Worth checking if you're above 60° latitude and skies are clear.
